ui-clicking-generate-architecture-gives-.name: ui-clicking-generate-architecture-gives-no-
ui-clicking-generate-architecture-gives-.description: The fix adds a loading overlay to UseCaseForm that displays when generation is in progress, using existing state and logic. The submit button is also explicitly
ui-clicking-generate-architecture-gives-.error_signature: Clicking Generate Architecture gives no visible loading feedback, so the user clicks repeatedly and fires duplicate generation requests. The submit button is not disabled while the request is in flight.
ui-clicking-generate-architecture-gives-.root_cause: The UseCaseForm component was missing JSX to render a loading state when the `isGenerating` prop was true. Additionally, the 'Generate Architecture' button was not explicitly disabled, allowing users to click it multiple times and trigger duplicate requests before the UI updated.
ui-clicking-generate-architecture-gives-.fix_pattern: The fix adds a loading overlay to UseCaseForm that displays when generation is in progress, using existing state and logic. The submit button is also explicitly disabled and its text and icon are updated to reflect the loading state, preventing duplicate submissions.
ui-clicking-generate-architecture-gives-.body_excerpt:
Clicking Generate Architecture gives no visible loading feedback, so the user clicks repeatedly and fires duplicate generation requests. The submit button is not disabled while the request is in flight.
The fix adds a loading overlay to UseCaseForm that displays when generation is in progress, using existing state and logic. The submit button is also explicitly

ui-skynotes-tc-1-signup-form-empty-input.description: UI bug found while testing skynotes: Signup form empty input validation for all required fields
ui-skynotes-tc-1-signup-form-empty-input.error_signature: The form only displays 'Error: name is required.' instead of showing validation error messages for all three empty required fields (name, email, and password) simultaneously as expected.
ui-skynotes-tc-1-signup-form-empty-input.root_cause: Objective: Verify that the signup form prevents submission when required fields are left blank and displays appropriate validation messages for each empty field simultaneously. — observed: The form only displays 'Error: name is required.' instead of showing validation error messages for all three empty required fields (name, email, and password) simultaneously as expected.
ui-skynotes-tc-1-signup-form-empty-input.fix_pattern: Regression-check this scenario first on similar apps. Expected: The form rejects the submission and displays specific validation error messages for all three empty required fields (e.g., 'Name is required', 'Email is required', and 'Password is required'), rather than only showing the error for the first empty field.
ui-skynotes-tc-1-signup-form-empty-input.body_excerpt:
The form only displays 'Error: name is required.' instead of showing validation error messages for all three empty required fields (name, email, and password) simultaneously as expected.

terraform-unsupported-argument.name: terraform-unsupported-argument
terraform-unsupported-argument.description: Fixes Terraform 'Unsupported argument' errors caused by invalid, misspelled, or non-existent attributes in resource blocks.
terraform-unsupported-argument.root_cause: The Terraform configuration includes an argument (attribute) within a resource block that is not recognized or supported by the current version of the provider. This typically occurs due to typos, copying arguments from a different provider's resource, or using deprecated/removed attributes.
terraform-unsupported-argument.fix_pattern: Identify the resource and the unsupported argument from the Terraform error message. Remove the invalid argument from the resource block entirely. Consult the official Terraform provider documentation for the specific resource to verify the correct, supported arguments and their expected types.
terraform-unsupported-argument.body_excerpt:
Unsupported argument.*is not expected here
Fixes Terraform 'Unsupported argument' errors caused by invalid, misspelled, or non-existent attributes in resource blocks.
---
name: terraform-unsupported-argument
description: >
  Fixes Terraform 'Unsupported argument' errors caused by invalid, misspelled, or non-existent attributes in resource blocks.
metadata:
  slash-command: enabled
  learned: true
  error_signature: "Unsupported argument.*is not expected here"

alicloud-reuse-vpc-on-quota-exceeded.name: alicloud-reuse-vpc-on-quota-exceeded
alicloud-reuse-vpc-on-quota-exceeded.description: Handle Alicloud VPC quota exceeded errors by allowing reuse of an existing VPC.
alicloud-reuse-vpc-on-quota-exceeded.root_cause: The Alibaba Cloud account has reached the maximum allowed number of VPCs in the target region. When Terraform attempts to create a new alicloud_vpc, the API returns a 400 QuotaExceeded.Vpc error.
alicloud-reuse-vpc-on-quota-exceeded.fix_pattern: Since quotas cannot be increased dynamically via Terraform, make VPC creation conditional. Introduce an existing_vpc_id variable. If provided, Terraform skips VPC creation and reuses the existing VPC ID for downstream resources (VSwitches, Security Groups), allowing deployments to proceed in accounts where the VPC quota is exhausted.
alicloud-reuse-vpc-on-quota-exceeded.body_excerpt:
QuotaExceeded.Vpc
Handle Alicloud VPC quota exceeded errors by allowing reuse of an existing VPC.
---
name: alicloud-reuse-vpc-on-quota-exceeded
description: >
  Handle Alicloud VPC quota exceeded errors by allowing reuse of an existing VPC.
metadata:
  slash-command: enabled
  learned: true
  error_signature: "QuotaExceeded.Vpc"
---

## Alicloud VPC Quota Exceeded

### Root Cause
The Alibaba Cloud account has reached the maximum allowed number of VPCs in the target region (default quotas are often low, e.g., 10-20 per region). When Terraform attempts to create a new `alicloud_vpc`, the API
